title: eucalyptus camaldulensis subsp. minima
intro: Eucalyptus camaldulensis subsp. minima is a subspecies of the River Red Gum, a small tree or shrub native to Western Australia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eucalyptus camaldulensis subsp. minima include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils to harness its medicinal properties.
